question: Will the S&P 500 be above 6924.9999 on Apr 10, 2026 at 4pm EDT?
reasoning: The S&P 500 closed at 6,782.81 on April 8, 2026, just 142 points (2.1%) below the required threshold of 6,924.9999. Given the significant rally from geopolitical ceasefire optimism and strong historical performance following bearish sentiment readings, the index is highly likely to gain the remaining 2.1% within two trading days. With futures markets already showing continued momentum and Wall Street analysts maintaining year-end targets around 7,650, the probability of reaching 6,925+ by tomorrow's close is substantially higher than the 3% market price suggests.
key_factors: Current S&P 500 at 6,782.81 needs only 2.1% gain to reach target
Strong rally momentum from Iran-US ceasefire agreement
Extreme bearish sentiment (51.4%) historically leads to 16% average gains
Wall Street median year-end target of 7,650 implies continued upside
Oil prices dropping significantly reducing inflationary pressures
risks: Ceasefire is only two weeks and could break down
Iran already claiming US violated agreement could dampen sentiment
Futures showing slight decline after Wednesday's massive rally
Geopolitical tensions remain elevated in Middle East
Energy sector weakness could drag on broader market
